name: Aronnax
description: Aronnax, rendered in lush watercolor, is a man weathered by the sea and driven by scientific curiosity. His face, a canvas of sun-kissed ochre and muted rose, is framed by a thick, unruly beard of salt-and-pepper, tinged with hints of marine blue in the shadowed depths. Deep-set eyes, a piercing aquamarine, are perpetually squinting as if gauging the horizon, etched with fine lines reflecting years spent battling wind and wave. A prominent nose, painted in shades of terracotta and mauve, hints at a past broken and reset, perhaps from a rogue wave or a shipboard brawl. He favors practical, layered clothing: a worn leather jerkin stained with sepia-toned salt spray over a faded indigo linen shirt, sleeves often rolled to reveal sinewy forearms daubed with subtle greens and browns from sea flora contact. His trousers are a sturdy canvas, a patchwork of muted grays and browns mirroring the ocean depths, tucked into knee-high, russet-colored boots stained with algae and seawater, all unified under a palette of oceanic blues, earthy browns, and sun-bleached yellows, reflecting his constant immersion in the marine world.
